skill.name: standup-meeting
skill.description: Conduct effective daily standup meetings for agile teams. Use when facilitating standups, tracking blockers, or improving team synchronization. Handles standup format, time management, and blocker resolution.
skill.summary: A skill for conducting effective daily standup meetings with templates for 3-questions format, board-based walkthroughs, and async updates.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 18 static findings are false positives. The skill is a pure markdown documentation file containing standup meeting templates. Backtick patterns detected are markdown code fences (```), not shell command execution. URL references are legitimate Scrum methodology documentation sites. No executable code, scripts, or network calls present.

low_findings.title: External Reference URLs
low_findings.description: Skill contains URLs to external Scrum documentation (scrumguides.org, mountaingoatsoftware.com). These are legitimate industry resources appropriate for an agile methodology skill.

content.value_statement: Running disorganized standups wastes team time and misses blockers. This skill provides structured templates and formats for efficient daily standups that keep agile teams synchronized.
actual_capabilities: Create board-based standup walkthrough templates for sprint board visualization
Produce async standup templates for remote and distributed teams
Format standup meeting minutes with action items and summaries
Enforce standup constraints (15-minute timebox, no problem-solving)
Track and highlight blockers with assigned owners and ETAs
limitations: Does not integrate with project management tools (Jira, Trello, etc.)
Does not automatically fetch task status from external systems
Does not send notifications or reminders to team members
Requires manual input of team updates and blocker information

use_cases.title: Daily Team Synchronization
use_cases.description: Use the 3-questions format to run efficient daily standups where each team member shares yesterday's work, today's plans, and any blockers.
use_cases.target_user: Scrum masters and team leads managing daily standups

use_cases.title: Sprint Board Visualization
use_cases.description: Walk through the sprint board during standup to visualize progress, identify blocked items, and maintain focus on sprint goals.
use_cases.target_user: Agile teams using Kanban or Scrum boards

use_cases.title: Async Updates for Remote Teams
use_cases.description: Create written standup updates for teams across different time zones who cannot meet synchronously.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Daily Standup
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a daily standup meeting using the 3-questions format. Include team member updates with yesterday, today, and blockers sections. Add action items with owners and due dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginning a new daily standup session

prompt_templates.title: Sprint Board Walkthrough
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a board-based standup. Walk through each column (Backlog, Ready, In Progress, Blocked, Done). Highlight blocked items and identify owners to unblock them.

prompt_templates.title: Create Async Standup Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an async standup template for our remote team. Include sections for yesterday, today, blockers, and sprint progress. Format for Slack or team wiki.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up async standup for distributed team

prompt_templates.title: Generate Meeting Minutes
prompt_templates.prompt: Create standup meeting minutes with attendee count, duration, individual updates, blockers summary, and prioritized action items with owners.

output_examples.input: Run a daily standup for a team of 4 developers
output_examples.output:
## Daily Standup Template

**Date**: 2025-01-15
**Duration**: 15 minutes

### Team Member A
- **Yesterday**: Completed user authentication API, code reviews
- **Today**: Implement JWT refresh token, write unit tests
- **Blockers**: Need Redis documentation

### Team Member B
- **Yesterday**: Frontend form validation
- **Today**: Profile page UI implementation
- **Blockers**: None

**Action Items**: [List with owners and times]

output_examples.input: Create async standup format for Slack
output_examples.output:
[Daily Update - Team Name]

**Yesterday**
- Completed feature X
- Fixed bug Y

**Today**
- Working on feature Z

**Blockers**
- None

**Sprint Progress**: 5/13 story points

best_practices: Time-box strictly to 15 minutes to maintain focus and respect team time
Use the parking lot technique - deep discussions happen after standup with only relevant participants
Rotate facilitation to share ownership and develop team members
anti_patterns: Turning standup into a status report to management instead of team sync
Solving technical problems during standup instead of noting them for later
Starting late or skipping standups inconsistently breaks team rhythm

faq.question: How long should a standup meeting last?
faq.answer: A standup should be strictly time-boxed to 15 minutes. If discussions need more time, schedule a separate meeting with only relevant participants.

faq.question: Who should attend standup meetings?
faq.answer: All team members working on the sprint should attend. The Scrum master facilitates, but everyone participates with their updates.

faq.question: Should we stand during standup?
faq.answer: Yes, standing helps keep the meeting short and energetic. It is a best practice from the agile methodology.

faq.question: What if someone has no blockers?
faq.answer: They should explicitly state 'No blockers' rather than skipping the section. This confirms they were asked and have no impediments.

faq.question: Can standup be async?
faq.answer: Yes, for remote or distributed teams, async standups written in Slack or a team wiki work well. However, synchronous standups are preferred when possible for better team bonding.

faq.question: Should we solve problems in standup?
faq.answer: No, standup is for sharing status, not solving problems. Note the issue and who needs to discuss it, then handle it after the meeting with only necessary people.
